steps.title: Use the table for line h
steps.content: Line $h$ contains $(18,130)$, $(23,160)$, and $(26,178)$. Entering these in Desmos and running gives $m=6$ and $b=22$.

steps.title: Write line h
steps.content: The equation of line $h$ is $$y=6x+22.$$ You can also get this by slope: $\frac{160-130}{23-18}=6$, then $130=6(18)+b$, so $b=22$.

steps.title: Translate down 5 units
steps.content: Moving a line down $5$ units subtracts $5$ from every $y$-value, so line $k$ is $$y=6x+22-5=6x+17.$$

steps.title: Find the x-intercept
steps.content: At the $x$-intercept, $y=0$: $$0=6x+17$$ $$x=-\frac{17}{6}.$$ Therefore the intercept is $\left(-\frac{17}{6},0\right)$, which is choice D.
